response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial experience (founding GISCollective, building GIS apps for Green Map) and proven low-latency systems work in DLang (async I/O, fibers, services handling millions of requests per second), which maps conceptually to the routing pipeline domain. However, modern C++ is a hard requirement and their hands-on C/C++ work dates to 2015 or earlier, with recent years spent primarily in TypeScript/Node full-stack product work. There is no direct navigation/routing or routing-tile pipeline experience, so an interview would need to probe how transferable their systems and geospatial background really is.
strengths: Founded and maintains an open-source geo-data platform (GISCollective) with real-world users
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, Linux and Docker/Kubernetes experience
concerns: Modern C++ experience is stale (last professional C/C++ role ended in 2015); systems work was in DLang, not C++ or Rust
No direct navigation, routing, or map-tile pipeline experience
Recent roles are TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, a shift away from the systems-level focus this role requires
